mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances or the t-statistic for return difference between triggered and non-triggered periods is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized broad-based demand growth, which supports corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth periods yield negative median 60-day returns or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization, which directly boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive equity returns over 20 days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day returns in > 55% of cases during recovery regimes, or predictive R-squared falls below 0.01 in rolling validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 2.5 percent annually forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, compressing equity multiples and producing negative forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of high-inflation observations or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which sustains consumer spending power and yields positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative median returns or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for concurrent monetary policy shifts in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts signal rising household confidence and construction sector activity, which flows through to broader economic optimism and generates positive short-term equity returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases are followed by negative 5-day returns in > 58% of cases or the signal shows zero predictive power in walk-forward validation with Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production indicate a supply-side contraction that precedes earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Declining INDPRO periods yield positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances or the threshold condition fails to predict return direction with statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.
